NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE SUBSTANCE OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT ALLEGES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO PLEAD A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO GIVE PLAINTIFFS AN OPPORTUNITY TO CLARIFY WHICH PLAINTIFF(S) IS/ARE MAKING A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE NOT AWARDABLE TO THE HEIRS ON THEIR OWN CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL DEATH. (C.C.P. 377.34, 377.61.). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT B.E. DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-30-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 503

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT B.E. DEVELOPMENT CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN BY DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH THE REQUISITE MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO WARRANT THE IMPOSITION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT ACTED WITH THE REQUISITE MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO WARRANT THE IMPOSITION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. WHILE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT NEED TO PROVE HIS CASE FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AT THE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION STAGE, THE COURT TAKES THE HIGHER PROOF STANDARD INTO CONSIDERATION. (SEE AMERICAN AIRLINES, INC. V. SHEPPARD, MULLIN, RICHTER & HAMPTON (2002) 96 CAL.APP.4TH 1017; HOCH V. ALLIED-SIGNAL, INC. (1994) 24 CAL.APP.4TH 48.) DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EXHIBITS D AND E ARE SUSTAINED AND ITS OBJECTIONS TO EXHIBITS J AND K ARE OVERRULED. EVEN IF THE COURT WERE TO CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S EXHIBITS D AND E, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM WOULD BE GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. THE COURT FURTHER HEARD AND GRANTED PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST TO VACATE THE 5/2/2016 TRIAL DATE AND STAY THE CASE. PARTIES TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A STIPULATION AND PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S CONSIDERATION. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, REPORTER: SHEILA PHAM OF VERITEXT, CSR #13293

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Benjamin H. Adams of FARRISE LAW FIRM (800-748-6186 / 310-424-3355), appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs / moving parties Glenn Williams, individually and as successor in interest to decedent J.D. Williams, et al.
James L. Mink of SEDGWICK LLP (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / opposing party B.E. Development Corporation

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS GLENN WILLIAMS, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO DECEDENT J.D. WILLIAMS, ET AL.'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS OF DEFENDANT B.E. DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to tax costs of defendant B.E. Development Corporation is denied. Defendant may recover its amended claimed costs in the amount of $21,509.16. Defendant's costs are allowable and were incurred, reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation and reasonable in amount. Allocation is unwarranted. (Howard v. American Nat. Fire Ins. Co. (2010) 187 Cal.App.4th 498.) Plaintiffs' oral request for a stay is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR #8052 (Tel: 925-360-6065) (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James L Mink of SEDGWICK LLP (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / moving party B.E. Development Corporation
Simona A. Farrise (appeared by CourtCall) and Alina C. Guzman of FARRISE LAW FIRM (310-424-3355), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Betty Williams, individually and as successor in interest to decedent J.D. Williams, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT B.E. DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant B.E. Development Corp.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden by demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Defendant's objections to Exhibits D and E are sustained and its objections to Exhibits J and K are overruled. Even if the Court were to consider plaintiff's Exhibits D and E, summary adjudication of the punitive damages claim would be granted. Order signed in open court.
The Court further heard and granted plaintiffs' request to vacate the 5/2/2016 trial date and stay the case. Parties to prepare and submit a stipulation and proposed order for the Court's consideration. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Sheila Pham of VERITEXT, CSR #13293 (415-274-9977) (503/GLW)

Notice Of Motion For An Order (1) Compelling Defendant B.E. Development Corporation To Provide Further Substantive Responses To Plaintiffs Discovery, Including Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Production, And Requests For Admission; And (2) For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Benjamin H. Adams of FARRISE LAW FIRM (800-748-6186), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Betty Williams, Glenn Williams, et al.
James L. Mink of SEDGWICK LLP (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / opposing party B.E. Development Corporation

Ruling:
PLAINTIFFS BETTY WILLIAMS / GLENN WILLIAMS, ET AL.'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER (1) COMPELLING DEFENDANT B.E. DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION TO PROVIDE FURTHER SUBSTANTIVE RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS' DISCOVERY, INCLUDING SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, FORM INTERROGATORIES, REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION; AND (2) FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

Hearing held. The Court made the follow rulings: (1) Plaintiffs' Motion for an order compelling defendant B.E. Development Corporation to provide further substantive responses to plaintiffs' discovery is granted in part and withdrawn in part per parties' agreement. Defendant has agreed to provide subsequent responses to certain special interrogatories by 4pm, July 7, 2014. Defendant also will produce documents per plaintiffs' request by 4pm, July 7, 2014. (2) Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions is granted. Sanctions to be imposed per C.C.P. 2023.010, misuse of the discovery process -- the requests for production of documents, for admissions, for responses to interrogatories and for meaningful meet and confer to resolve discovery disputes. Plaintiffs' initial request of $4,610 as reasonable attorney's fees is denied, and the amount is reduced to $2,610. Sanctions are imposed in the amount of $1,000 per C.C.P. 2023.030(a) and 2023.010 for failure to respond to request for production of documents #69 in a timely manner. Discovery is to be reopened and limited as to the production of #69. Defendant is ordered to produce all documents and to be authenticated. The documents are to be produced by 7/18/2014 and discovery will be closed on that day. Sanctions are to be paid no later than 7/18/2014. Refer to reporter's transcript for details of ruling. Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Donna J. Blum, CSR #11133 (415-602-6910) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel D. O'Shea of PERKINS COIE (310-788-9900), appearing for defendant / moving party Honeywell International, Inc. F/K/A Alliedsignal Inc., Successor-In-Interest to The Bendix Corporation
Benjamin H. Adams and Briana E. McCarthy of FARRISE LAW FIRM (800-748-6186), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Betty Williams, Glenn Williams, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. F/K/A ALLIEDSIGNAL INC., SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO THE BENDIX CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Defendant Honeywell International, Inc. F/K/A Alliedsignal Inc., Successor-In-Interest to The Bendix Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that all causes of action alleged against it are time-barred. As to the survival causes of action, defendant failed to demonstrate that they are all time-barred by C.C.P. 366.1. As to the wrongful death causes of action, defendant failed to demonstrate they are time-barred by C.C.P. 340.2: Plaintiffs' initial complaint was filed within the statute of limitations period. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the amendment substituting defendant for a Doe does not relate back to the filing of the initial complaint because it failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs were not genuinely ignorant of defendant's true name or the facts rendering it liable when the complaint was filed. Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION Defendant Autozone West, Inc.S Notice Of Motiona And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Wrongful Death (Asbestos)
Off Calendar Mar-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Autozone West, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' first amended complaint is denied in part and granted in part. Defendant's motion is denied as to the substance of plaintiffs' complaint. Plaintiffs' complaint alleges facts sufficient to plead a claim for punitive damages. Defendant's motion is granted with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity to clarify which plaintiff(s) is/are making a claim for punitive damages. Punitive damages are not awardable to the heirs on their own cause of action for wrongful death. (C.C.P. 377.34, 377.61.). Prevailing party to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
